words.definition: More than enough of something.
words.sentence_usage: Abundant berries hung from the hedge, turning the lane into a feast of colour.

words.definition: To use something wrongly or to treat someone cruelly.
words.sentence_usage: You should not abuse your privileges at school.

words.definition: To accept or admit that something is true or exists.
words.sentence_usage: She paused to acknowledge the help from her team.

words.definition: Upset and unable to stay calm.
words.sentence_usage: The agitated horse stamped the ground as thunder rolled across the hills.

words.definition: To claim something without proving it yet.
words.sentence_usage: Witnesses allege that the contract was signed under pressure.

words.definition: To give something to someone for a particular purpose.
words.sentence_usage: The council will allocate extra funds to the library.

words.definition: Up in the air or held high.
words.sentence_usage: The kite soared aloft above the hill, tugging hard at the string.

words.definition: A table or raised place used in religious ceremonies.
words.sentence_usage: Flowers were placed beside the altar before the service.

words.definition: A person who does something for enjoyment rather than as a profession.
words.sentence_usage: Although an amateur, she played the violin beautifully.

words.definition: Friendly and pleasant to be around.
words.sentence_usage: The amiable shopkeeper greeted every child with a warm smile and a cheerful joke.

words.definition: To make something louder, stronger, or greater.
words.sentence_usage: The microphone will amplify your voice in the hall.

words.definition: A comparison that explains one thing by showing how it is like another.
words.sentence_usage: The teacher used an analogy about traffic to explain blood flow.

words.definition: An excited feeling about something that is about to happen.
words.sentence_usage: In breathless anticipation, the class waited for the theatre curtain to rise.

words.definition: The highest point or tip of something.
words.sentence_usage: The climbers reached the apex of the ridge at noon.

words.definition: Very difficult and requiring a lot of effort.
words.sentence_usage: Training for the marathon was an arduous process.

words.definition: An object made by humans, especially one of historical interest.
words.sentence_usage: The museum displayed an ancient artefact from Egypt.

words.definition: Very surprised.
words.sentence_usage: Mia stared at the floating lanterns, too astonished to say a single word.

words.definition: To help or be of use.
words.sentence_usage: All our planning will avail little without teamwork.

words.definition: A digital image or character representing a user online.
words.sentence_usage: He designed a dragon avatar for the game.

words.definition: A concentrated outpouring, such as of criticism or gunfire.
words.sentence_usage: The minister faced a barrage of questions from reporters.

words.definition: A barrier built to block movement or protect an area.
words.sentence_usage: Workers set up a barricade around the unsafe stairs.

words.definition: Aggressive and ready to argue or fight.
words.sentence_usage: The belligerent leader delivered a fiery speech that made the crowded chamber fall into an uneasy silence.

words.definition: Shouted in a very loud voice.
words.sentence_usage: The giant bellowed across the valley, making pebbles dance on the path.

words.definition: Very confused and unsure what is happening.
words.sentence_usage: The bewildered explorer spun in circles when every path in the cave looked the same.

words.definition: An unfair preference for or against something.
words.sentence_usage: Good research tries to remove personal bias.

words.definition: Cold, empty, and without much hope.
words.sentence_usage: A bleak wind swept across the moor, carrying whispers through the dry grass.

words.definition: To burst into flowers or grow beautifully.
words.sentence_usage: With a splash of spring rain, the orchard began to blossom overnight.

words.definition: Quick, lively, and full of energy.
words.sentence_usage: They set off at a brisk pace before the sun could melt the silver frost.

words.definition: To move slightly or to change an opinion.
words.sentence_usage: The heavy sofa would not budge.

words.definition: Full of busy movement and noise.
words.sentence_usage: The bustling market overflowed with voices, spices, and clattering carts.

words.definition: A horrible, loud mix of messy noises.
words.sentence_usage: The peaceful morning was shattered by a sudden cacophony of screeching tires, blaring horns, and shouting voices.

words.definition: A terrible event that causes lots of trouble.
words.sentence_usage: What began as a prank soon turned into a calamity when the paint spilled across the stage.

words.definition: A deep valley with steep sides, often carved by a river.
words.sentence_usage: They looked down into the vast canyon below.

words.definition: So interesting or beautiful that it holds all your attention.
words.sentence_usage: The storyteller's captivating voice made the campfire shadows seem alive.

words.definition: Careful to avoid danger or mistakes.
words.sentence_usage: Cautious steps carried the children across the icy bridge above the roaring stream.

words.definition: Never stopping.
words.sentence_usage: The ceaseless tapping of rain on the roof kept everyone awake through the night.

words.definition: A place where people are buried.
words.sentence_usage: The old cemetery is beside the church.

words.definition: An official count of a population.
words.sentence_usage: The national census is completed every ten years.

words.definition: Very messy, wild, and out of control.
words.sentence_usage: Books, socks, and half-finished maps covered the floor in chaotic heaps.

words.definition: To move around continuously in a system or area.
words.sentence_usage: Fresh air must circulate through the classroom.

words.definition: A condition or fact connected with an event.
words.sentence_usage: Under these circumstances, we should wait.

words.definition: Climbed with hands and feet in an awkward way.
words.sentence_usage: He clambered over the fallen tree trunk to reach the hidden nest.

words.definition: Done in secret so nobody notices.
words.sentence_usage: They held a clandestine meeting behind the curtain while the audience applauded.

words.definition: An exact copy of a living thing or digital system.
words.sentence_usage: Scientists produced a clone of the plant.

words.definition: To suddenly fall down.
words.sentence_usage: With a groan of splintering wood, the old shed began to collapse.

words.definition: Shared or done by a group together.
words.sentence_usage: The team made a collective decision.

words.definition: An impact between moving objects.
words.sentence_usage: Two bikes were damaged in the collision.

words.definition: A noisy fuss with lots of movement.
words.sentence_usage: A sudden commotion in the playground sent teachers hurrying across the field.

words.definition: A kind wish to help someone who is hurting.
words.sentence_usage: Her compassion showed when she wrapped her scarf around the shivering puppy.

words.definition: To force or strongly persuade someone to do something.
words.sentence_usage: Evidence may compel the committee to act.

words.definition: To make up for loss, damage, or extra effort.
words.sentence_usage: The company will compensate workers for overtime.

words.definition: Hidden so it cannot easily be seen.
words.sentence_usage: A concealed lever behind the painting opened the secret staircase.

words.definition: General agreement among a group.
words.sentence_usage: The panel reached a consensus after debate.

words.definition: A focus on buying goods and services in large amounts.
words.sentence_usage: The documentary criticized modern consumerism.

words.definition: To think deeply about something.
words.sentence_usage: He sat quietly to contemplate his options.

words.definition: Quietly happy and satisfied.
words.sentence_usage: The cat looked contented as it purred beside the warm kitchen stove.

words.definition: Likely to cause strong disagreement or argument.
words.sentence_usage: The contentious debate over the new law split the council and filled the town hall with angry voices.

words.definition: The circumstances that help explain meaning.
words.sentence_usage: Without context, the quote seems misleading.

words.definition: A widely accepted way of doing something or a large meeting.
words.sentence_usage: By convention, we start the meeting at nine.

words.definition: A strong belief or a formal declaration of guilt in court.
words.sentence_usage: She spoke with conviction about climate action.

words.definition: Related to beauty treatment or superficial appearance.
words.sentence_usage: The damage was cosmetic and did not affect safety.

words.definition: Very brave.
words.sentence_usage: With one courageous leap, Zara crossed the rushing stream and pulled her friend to safety.

words.definition: A small sheltered bay or coastal inlet.
words.sentence_usage: They anchored the boat in a quiet cove.

words.definition: A deep, rich red colour.
words.sentence_usage: Crimson leaves spun through the courtyard like sparks from a fire.

words.definition: Fresh, cool, and pleasantly sharp.
words.sentence_usage: A crisp autumn breeze scattered gold leaves across the playground.

words.definition: To make someone feel intimidated or discouraged.
words.sentence_usage: The steep climb did not daunt the hikers.

words.definition: Extremely bright or impressive.
words.sentence_usage: The dazzling chandelier sprayed diamonds of light around the ballroom.

words.definition: Boldly refusing to obey.
words.sentence_usage: The defiant robin stayed on the gate even as the dog barked below.

words.definition: Fine, light, and easy to damage.
words.sentence_usage: A delicate layer of frost turned every blade of grass into crystal.

words.definition: A false belief held despite strong evidence against it.
words.sentence_usage: The doctor treated his delusion with care.

words.definition: To be a sign of or to indicate clearly.
words.sentence_usage: A red circle may denote danger.

words.definition: Empty, lonely, and sad.
words.sentence_usage: The desolate beach stretched for miles without a single footprint in the sand.

words.definition: Fully decided to keep going and not give up.
words.sentence_usage: Determined to finish the race, Arjun pushed through the mud and rain.

words.definition: Something that discourages a particular action.
words.sentence_usage: Visible cameras act as a deterrent to theft.

words.definition: Deeply committed to a faith or belief.
words.sentence_usage: Her devout grandfather attended daily prayers.

words.definition: A regional or social variety of a language.
words.sentence_usage: His dialect includes words unique to the area.

words.definition: Old, broken, and falling apart from being ignored for a long time.
words.sentence_usage: At the end of the lane sat a dilapidated cottage, its roof caved in and windows shattered by time.

words.definition: Hard-working and careful.
words.sentence_usage: The diligent apprentice polished every brass handle until it gleamed like gold.

words.definition: To treat people unfairly based on group differences.
words.sentence_usage: It is illegal to discriminate in hiring.

words.definition: To spread out in different directions.
words.sentence_usage: At the blast of the whistle, the pigeons began to disperse into the pale morning sky.

words.definition: To change direction away from the usual course.
words.sentence_usage: Police diverted traffic after the accident.

words.definition: Related to the home or to one’s own country.
words.sentence_usage: Domestic flights are usually shorter than international ones.

words.definition: Extreme and likely to have a strong effect.
words.sentence_usage: The team made drastic changes to improve results.

words.definition: Dull, grey, and a bit depressing.
words.sentence_usage: A dreary afternoon hung over the town, with clouds smudging the sky like charcoal.

words.definition: Moved slowly and lightly.
words.sentence_usage: Feathers drifted through the open window and settled on the desk.

words.definition: In the proper way or at the expected time.
words.sentence_usage: The form was duly signed and submitted.

words.definition: The length of time something lasts.
words.sentence_usage: The exam duration is ninety minutes.

words.definition: Very ready and excited to do something.
words.sentence_usage: The eager pupils rushed to the window when snowflakes started to fall.

words.definition: Related to life on Earth rather than spiritual matters.
words.sentence_usage: He worried about earthly problems instead of ideals.

words.definition: Strange and spooky in a way that makes you uneasy.
words.sentence_usage: An eerie silence settled over the forest after the owl's sudden cry.

words.definition: Extremely happy and proud.
words.sentence_usage: Elated by her surprise victory, Nina skipped all the way home.

words.definition: To injure or kill with an electric shock.
words.sentence_usage: Never touch exposed wires; they can electrocute you.

words.definition: Graceful and stylish.
words.sentence_usage: The swan glided in an elegant curve across the moonlit lake.

words.definition: A rich, deep green colour.
words.sentence_usage: Emerald light filtered through the leaves and painted the path below.

words.definition: To express the main idea briefly or to enclose something.
words.sentence_usage: The slogan encapsulates the spirit of the campaign.

words.definition: Extremely angry.
words.sentence_usage: Enraged by the broken promise, the dragon slammed its tail against the cave floor.

words.definition: Something beautiful that lasts for only a very short time, like a bubble.
words.sentence_usage: The beautiful sunset was an ephemeral masterpiece, fading into darkness almost as quickly as it had appeared.

words.definition: A widespread outbreak of disease in a community or region.
words.sentence_usage: Health officials acted quickly to control the epidemic.

words.definition: The practice of spying to obtain secret information.
words.sentence_usage: The novel follows a network involved in espionage.

words.definition: Avoiding clear answers or action.
words.sentence_usage: His evasive reply made everyone suspicious.

words.definition: A short passage taken from a longer text.
words.sentence_usage: We read an excerpt from the novel in class.

words.definition: To be a typical example of something.
words.sentence_usage: Her patience exemplifies good leadership.

words.definition: Special skill or deep knowledge in a subject.
words.sentence_usage: We asked for her expertise in data security.

words.definition: Extremely beautiful and carefully made.
words.sentence_usage: An exquisite pattern of ice spread across the window like silver lace.

words.definition: Costing too much or more than reasonable.
words.sentence_usage: The hotel looked beautiful but extravagant.

words.definition: Grown weaker, paler, or less clear.
words.sentence_usage: The faded sign still pointed towards the harbour, although half its paint had peeled away.

words.definition: Hesitated or lost strength for a moment.
words.sentence_usage: His voice faltered when he heard footsteps echoing behind him in the tunnel.

words.definition: Very fierce and wild.
words.sentence_usage: The ferocious storm clawed at the windows with wind and rain.

words.definition: A person who holds a title but little real power.
words.sentence_usage: The chairperson became a figurehead after the merger.

words.definition: Having limits; not endless.
words.sentence_usage: Our time in the lab is finite, so work carefully.

words.definition: Shone or moved unsteadily.
words.sentence_usage: Candlelight flickered across the tunnel walls and made the shadows dance.

words.definition: To grow or do very well.
words.sentence_usage: With sunshine and patience, the tiny seedlings began to flourish in the school garden.

words.definition: To lose or give up something as a penalty.
words.sentence_usage: The team may forfeit the match if they arrive late.

words.definition: Lonely and sad because something is missing.
words.sentence_usage: A forlorn whistle drifted from the station after the last train had gone.

words.definition: To abandon or leave someone or something behind.
words.sentence_usage: He chose not to forsake his old friends.

words.definition: Bad-tempered and difficult to keep under control.
words.sentence_usage: As shortages worsened, the normally orderly crowd became fractious and impossible to soothe.

words.definition: Easily broken or likely to fail if not handled carefully.
words.sentence_usage: The negotiators knew the fragile agreement could collapse with a single careless decision.

words.definition: Having a sweet or pleasant smell.
words.sentence_usage: Fragrant roses climbed the gate and filled the air with summer sweetness.

words.definition: Wild with worry, fear, or hurry.
words.sentence_usage: Frantic footsteps thundered upstairs as everyone searched for the missing key.

words.definition: Filled with stress, danger, or difficulty.
words.sentence_usage: Their fraught journey through the besieged city was slowed by fear, rubble, and warning sirens.

words.definition: To provide furniture or to supply what is needed.
words.sentence_usage: They will furnish the flat before renting it.

words.definition: Extremely huge.
words.sentence_usage: A gargantuan shadow rose from the sea and blocked the setting sun.

words.definition: A faint, small light.
words.sentence_usage: A glimmer of gold shone beneath the dusty floorboards.

words.definition: Shining with a wet or polished sparkle.
words.sentence_usage: Glistening raindrops clung to the spider web like tiny pearls.

words.definition: Dark, sad, or without much cheer.
words.sentence_usage: The gloomy corridor seemed to swallow every sound except the drip of water.

words.definition: Wonderful, splendid, and full of beauty.
words.sentence_usage: It was a glorious morning, with birdsong rising through the clear blue air.

words.definition: Twisted and rough, usually because of age.
words.sentence_usage: A gnarled oak tree crouched beside the path like an old giant.

words.definition: Moving in a smooth and lovely way.
words.sentence_usage: The graceful deer leapt over the brook without disturbing a single reed.

words.definition: Serious, dark, and worrying.
words.sentence_usage: A grim expression spread across the captain's face as the fog swallowed the lighthouse.

words.definition: To gain unauthorized access to a computer system.
words.sentence_usage: Criminals tried to hack the school database.

words.definition: Excessive speed that can cause mistakes.
words.sentence_usage: In her haste, she left her keys on the bus.

words.definition: Seeming to be visited by ghosts or troubled memories.
words.sentence_usage: The haunted house loomed over the village with boarded windows and a leaning porch.

words.definition: Warm, strong, and full of energy or enthusiasm.
words.sentence_usage: A hearty laugh burst from Grandad as the puppy chased its own tail.

words.definition: Slow to act because you are unsure.
words.sentence_usage: She gave a hesitant knock before pushing open the office door.

words.definition: A system where people or things are ranked in levels.
words.sentence_usage: The company hierarchy has clear reporting lines.

words.definition: A picture-based symbol used in ancient Egyptian writing.
words.sentence_usage: The wall was covered with carved hieroglyphs.

words.definition: Empty inside or echoing.
words.sentence_usage: A hollow knock came from the trapdoor beneath the carpet.

words.definition: Friendly and generous treatment of guests.
words.sentence_usage: We appreciated the family’s warm hospitality.

words.definition: Unfriendly and ready to oppose or attack.
words.sentence_usage: The hostile exchange between the rival groups made the square feel dangerous and unstable.

words.definition: Crowded closely together for warmth or safety.
words.sentence_usage: The chicks huddled beneath their mother when the first drops of rain fell.

words.definition: Having a lot of moisture in the air.
words.sentence_usage: It felt hot and humid before the storm.

words.definition: A proposed explanation for something that can be tested.
words.sentence_usage: The scientist formed a hypothesis before beginning her experiment.

words.definition: Impossible or very difficult to read.
words.sentence_usage: His handwriting was so messy it was almost illegible.

words.definition: Forbidden by law or accepted rules.
words.sentence_usage: Police uncovered an illicit trade in stolen phones.

words.definition: Lit up brightly.
words.sentence_usage: The moon illuminated the ruined tower, turning every stone silver-white.

words.definition: Extremely large.
words.sentence_usage: An immense door of carved oak swung slowly open on its rusty hinges.

words.definition: Living or lasting forever; never dying.
words.sentence_usage: The ancient Greeks believed their gods were immortal.

words.definition: Unable to wait calmly.
words.sentence_usage: Impatient to begin, Leo tapped his pencil against the desk like a tiny drum.

words.definition: Not allowing liquid or gas to pass through.
words.sentence_usage: The impermeable membrane kept the water from seeping through.

words.definition: So good, big, or clever that it makes people admire it.
words.sentence_usage: The castle's impressive towers rose above the valley like stone spears.

words.definition: To put or keep someone in prison.
words.sentence_usage: The judge decided to imprison the thief for two years.

words.definition: Shining with a bright, glowing light.
words.sentence_usage: The incandescent lava lit the cavern with a dangerous orange glow.

words.definition: Unable to exist or work together in harmony.
words.sentence_usage: Their personalities were completely incompatible, so they argued constantly.

words.definition: Not staying the same; changing or varying.
words.sentence_usage: His inconsistent effort meant his grades kept going up and down.

words.definition: Too unusual or extreme to be described in words.
words.sentence_usage: The view from the summit was indescribable in its beauty.

words.definition: Not fit or safe to be eaten.
words.sentence_usage: The mushrooms turned out to be inedible and were thrown away.

words.definition: Designed to stir up anger or strong feelings.
words.sentence_usage: One inflammatory remark was enough to send the already tense meeting into chaos.

words.definition: A change in the tone or pitch of the voice; also a change in word form.
words.sentence_usage: She used different inflections to show whether she was asking a question or making a statement.

words.definition: To fill something with a quality or to soak a substance to extract its flavour.
words.sentence_usage: She left the herbs to infuse in hot water to make a soothing tea.

words.definition: Very clever and inventive.
words.sentence_usage: Her ingenious plan used mirrors to bounce sunlight into the gloomy attic.

words.definition: Words carved, printed, or written on a surface.
words.sentence_usage: There was a Latin inscription carved into the stone arch of the cathedral.

words.definition: A clear and deep understanding of something.
words.sentence_usage: Reading widely gave her great insight into human nature.

words.definition: Cheerful and friendly.
words.sentence_usage: The jovial baker hummed a tune as he handed out warm buns.

words.definition: Bursting with joy and celebration.
words.sentence_usage: The team was jubilant when the winning goal rippled the net.

words.definition: Extremely tired.
words.sentence_usage: After the charity run, even the most talkative pupils were completely knackered.

words.definition: Like a maze with many twisting turns.
words.sentence_usage: Labyrinthine corridors curled beneath the palace, confusing every visitor who entered.

words.definition: To show sadness about a loss.
words.sentence_usage: The villagers gathered to lament the great oak that had fallen in the storm.

words.definition: A temporary failure or a period of time passing.
words.sentence_usage: A momentary lapse in concentration caused him to make the mistake.

words.definition: Rich, grand, and more than enough.
words.sentence_usage: A lavish banquet covered the table with roasted meats, fruits, and sparkling drinks.

words.definition: Allowed by the law; legal.
words.sentence_usage: It is not lawful to drive without a valid licence.

words.definition: Something left behind by a person, such as money or an achievement.
words.sentence_usage: The scientist's greatest legacy was her discovery of a cure for the disease.

words.definition: Stayed longer than expected.
words.sentence_usage: The smell of cinnamon lingered in the kitchen long after the pies had cooled.

words.definition: A means of earning money in order to live.
words.sentence_usage: Farming was his family's main livelihood for generations.

words.definition: Appearing large, dark, and threatening.
words.sentence_usage: A looming cliff rose above the boat, hiding the sky from view.

words.definition: Glowing brightly in the dark.
words.sentence_usage: The cave was bathed in a luminous, ethereal glow emanating from the strange crystals on the ceiling.

words.definition: Moved suddenly and awkwardly.
words.sentence_usage: The bus lurched forward, sending backpacks sliding across the floor.

words.definition: Grand and beautiful in a powerful way.
words.sentence_usage: A majestic eagle circled above the valley, silent and sure.

words.definition: An official order or authority to do something.
words.sentence_usage: The government was given a mandate by voters to reform the health service.

words.definition: A word or phrase repeated often, especially in meditation or as a belief.
words.sentence_usage: Her personal mantra was to never give up, no matter the challenge.

words.definition: An environment or framework in which something develops; also a grid of numbers.
words.sentence_usage: The city's road network formed a complex matrix of routes.

words.definition: Moved or followed a path with many gentle bends.
words.sentence_usage: The stream meandered through the meadow like a ribbon of glass.

words.definition: A quiet, heavy sadness that lasts a long time.
words.sentence_usage: The constant, drumming rain matched the melancholy mood that hung heavily over the deserted town.

words.definition: Something built or done to honour the memory of a person or event.
words.sentence_usage: A stone memorial was erected in the town square to remember those lost in the war.

words.definition: Looking as if it could cause danger.
words.sentence_usage: Menacing clouds gathered over the sea and swallowed the last patch of blue.

words.definition: Playfully naughty.
words.sentence_usage: A mischievous grin spread across Ben's face as he hid the treasure map.

words.definition: Very unhappy and uncomfortable.
words.sentence_usage: Soaked by the storm, the campers looked miserable beneath the flapping tent.

words.definition: To understand or explain something incorrectly.
words.sentence_usage: She misinterpreted his silence as rudeness when he was simply thinking.

words.definition: Filled with light fog.
words.sentence_usage: A misty veil drifted over the lake and softened the shapes of the boats.

words.definition: Dark and hard to see through.
words.sentence_usage: Murky water swirled around the old pier and hid what lay below.

words.definition: Spoke in a low, unclear voice.
words.sentence_usage: He muttered a worried warning as the floorboards creaked beneath them.

words.definition: Difficult to explain or understand.
words.sentence_usage: A mysterious package appeared on the doorstep without a name or note.

words.definition: To fail to care for someone or something properly.
words.sentence_usage: If you neglect your plants and forget to water them, they will wilt.

words.definition: Quick and light in movement.
words.sentence_usage: The nimble fox darted between tree roots and vanished into the ferns.

words.definition: Active at night.
words.sentence_usage: Nocturnal creatures stirred in the hedges as the village lamps blinked on.

words.definition: Feeling warm but sad when remembering the past.
words.sentence_usage: The crackling tune made Grandma nostalgic for summers by the seaside.

words.definition: Famous for something bad.
words.sentence_usage: The notorious pirate was whispered about in every harbour tavern.

words.definition: Harmful or very unpleasant, especially in smell.
words.sentence_usage: Noxious smoke curled from the cauldron and stung everyone's eyes.

words.definition: Willing to do as you are told.
words.sentence_usage: The obedient dog sat at once when its owner raised a hand.

words.definition: Hard to see, notice, or understand.
words.sentence_usage: An obscure symbol was scratched into the wall behind the curtain.

words.definition: An idea or feeling that occupies the mind too much.
words.sentence_usage: His obsession with football meant he thought of little else.

words.definition: Giving the feeling that something bad is about to happen.
words.sentence_usage: An ominous rumble rolled beneath the ground before the cave began to shake.

words.definition: Happening at a particularly suitable or favourable time.
words.sentence_usage: Her arrival at an opportune moment helped solve the problem quickly.

words.definition: Rich, luxurious, and expensive-looking.
words.sentence_usage: Opulent curtains of velvet framed the stage like a royal cloak.

words.definition: Decorated with lots of fancy detail.
words.sentence_usage: An ornate mirror hung above the fireplace, edged with carved roses and birds.

words.definition: Extremely happy.
words.sentence_usage: She was overjoyed when the lost necklace glittered from the flower bed.

words.definition: The inner surface of the hand; also a tropical tree.
words.sentence_usage: She held the small bird gently in the palm of her hand.

words.definition: A wide, unbroken view of a large area.
words.sentence_usage: From the hilltop, there was a breathtaking panorama of the valley below.

words.definition: Accepting what happens without taking action; not active.
words.sentence_usage: He took a passive role in the discussion, letting others make the decisions.

words.definition: Strange in an interesting way.
words.sentence_usage: A peculiar humming noise drifted from the clock at midnight.

words.definition: The way in which something is understood or interpreted by the senses or mind.
words.sentence_usage: His perception of the situation was different from everyone else's.

words.definition: Very dangerous.
words.sentence_usage: The climbers edged along the perilous ledge above the crashing waves.

words.definition: Confused because something is difficult to understand.
words.sentence_usage: Jaden looked perplexed when the map showed a road straight through the lake.

words.definition: Keeping going and refusing to give up.
words.sentence_usage: A persistent drizzle followed them all the way to the mountain hut.

words.definition: So incredibly scared that you freeze up like a stone statue.
words.sentence_usage: Rooted to the spot, the young boy stood entirely petrified as the shadow detached itself from the wall.

words.definition: Very sharp, bright, or strong.
words.sentence_usage: A piercing scream shattered the silence of the sleeping house.

words.definition: Calm and peaceful, without strong movement or emotion.
words.sentence_usage: The pond lay placid beneath the dawn, smooth as polished glass.

words.definition: To use someone else's work or ideas and present them as your own.
words.sentence_usage: It is dishonest to plagiarise another author's writing.

words.definition: A flat area of high land; also a period when there is no progress.
words.sentence_usage: The explorers reached a wide plateau after climbing the steep slope.

words.definition: Balanced, calm, and ready.
words.sentence_usage: The gymnast stood poised on the beam before springing into the air.

words.definition: Unsteady or unsafe because it could suddenly get worse.
words.sentence_usage: Peace remained precarious, with both sides watching each other from behind the shattered barricades.

words.definition: Very valuable or loved.
words.sentence_usage: She tucked the precious letter safely inside a wooden box.

words.definition: The act of keeping something safe from harm or decay.
words.sentence_usage: The preservation of ancient buildings is important for future generations.

words.definition: Covered in sharp points or easily annoyed.
words.sentence_usage: A prickly hedge guarded the narrow path to the orchard.

words.definition: Happening or developing gradually over time; favouring new ideas.
words.sentence_usage: The school took a progressive approach to teaching, using technology in every lesson.

words.definition: Important, well known, or easily seen.
words.sentence_usage: The cathedral was the most prominent building in the city skyline.

words.definition: Very pleased with yourself or someone else.
words.sentence_usage: Proud of her painting, Eva carried it home as carefully as treasure.

words.definition: The action of supplying something; also a condition in an agreement.
words.sentence_usage: The charity focused on the provision of food to those in need.

words.definition: Nearness in space, time, or relationship.
words.sentence_usage: The school was chosen because of its proximity to their home.

words.definition: Very small and weak.
words.sentence_usage: The puny candle flame could not warm the vast, draughty hall.

words.definition: Decaying and having a foul smell.
words.sentence_usage: The putrid smell from the bin told them it needed emptying urgently.

words.definition: Charmingly old-fashioned.
words.sentence_usage: A quaint row of cottages lined the lane with crooked chimneys and flower boxes.

words.definition: Shook with small quick movements.
words.sentence_usage: The leaf quivered when the first raindrop landed upon it.

words.definition: Shining brightly or looking full of happiness.
words.sentence_usage: Her radiant smile brightened the room more quickly than the lamps.

words.definition: Rough, torn, or uneven.
words.sentence_usage: Ragged clouds crawled across the moon like ripped grey cloth.

words.definition: Based on reason and clear thinking, rather than emotion.
words.sentence_usage: He tried to remain rational despite feeling upset about the decision.

words.definition: Extremely hungry.
words.sentence_usage: After swimming for hours, the children were ravenous and cheered at the sight of sandwiches.

words.definition: A kingdom or a field of activity or knowledge.
words.sentence_usage: The wizard was the most powerful sorcerer in the realm.

words.definition: Making someone feel calmer and safer.
words.sentence_usage: Dad's reassuring nod gave her the courage to step onto the stage.

words.definition: To speak severely to someone because they have done something wrong.
words.sentence_usage: The teacher had to rebuke the pupils for talking during the exam.

words.definition: Doing dangerous things without thinking.
words.sentence_usage: With a reckless laugh, he rode straight into the muddy stream.

words.definition: To put something right; to correct a mistake.
words.sentence_usage: The company promised to rectify the error in the bill as soon as possible.

words.definition: A system of government; also a set of rules or a routine.
words.sentence_usage: The country suffered under a harsh military regime for many years.

words.definition: To restore someone to good health or a normal life; to restore something to good condition.
words.sentence_usage: The charity worked to rehabilitate injured birds before releasing them into the wild.

words.definition: Not wanting to do something.
words.sentence_usage: Reluctant to leave the fire, the campers pulled their blankets tighter.

words.definition: Able to recover quickly after difficulty.
words.sentence_usage: The resilient little plant pushed through the cracked pavement after the storm.

words.definition: Very determined and sure.
words.sentence_usage: Resolute and steady, the captain kept the ship pointed towards the light.

words.definition: A firm decision to do or not to do something; the quality of being determined.
words.sentence_usage: She made a New Year's resolution to read one book every month.

words.definition: Weak and shaky, as if it might fall apart.
words.sentence_usage: They tiptoed across the rickety bridge while the river foamed below.

words.definition: The quality of being thorough and careful; also harsh conditions.
words.sentence_usage: The rigour of the training programme prepared the athletes for competition.

words.definition: Moving in small waves.
words.sentence_usage: Rippling laughter spread through the hall when the magician dropped his hat.

words.definition: A religious or solemn ceremony or act.
words.sentence_usage: Lighting candles was an important rite in their religious tradition.

words.definition: Strong, healthy, and sturdy.
words.sentence_usage: A robust oak door protected the library from the howling wind.

words.definition: Making a soft swishing sound.
words.sentence_usage: Rustling leaves whispered above their heads as dusk crept in.

words.definition: A long story of adventure or a series of events.
words.sentence_usage: The film series told the saga of a family across three generations.

words.definition: Fierce, violent, and wild.
words.sentence_usage: Savage waves hammered the cliffs until white spray burst high into the air.

words.definition: A bright red colour.
words.sentence_usage: Scarlet petals floated across the pond like tiny boats of fire.

words.definition: To search an area for information; a person sent to gather information.
words.sentence_usage: She was sent to scout the area ahead before the rest of the team moved forward.

words.definition: To examine something very carefully.
words.sentence_usage: The inspector began to scrutinise every document in the file.

words.definition: Moved quickly with short, hurried steps.
words.sentence_usage: Mice scurried across the barn floor when the lantern flickered on.

words.definition: To produce and release a substance; also to hide something.
words.sentence_usage: Certain glands secrete hormones that regulate the body's functions.

words.definition: A distinct part or branch of something, such as an economy or area.
words.sentence_usage: The technology sector has grown rapidly over the past decade.

words.definition: A feeling or opinion about something; emotion rather than reason.
words.sentence_usage: There was a strong public sentiment against the new development.

words.definition: Calm and peaceful.
words.sentence_usage: The garden looked serene beneath the first light of dawn.

words.definition: Twisting and turning like a moving snake.
words.sentence_usage: The river carved a serpentine path through the dense, unforgiving jungle.

words.definition: Shining with a soft, wavering light.
words.sentence_usage: A shimmering curtain of heat rose above the desert road.

words.definition: Cried out in a loud, high voice.
words.sentence_usage: She shrieked when the icy water splashed over the side of the boat.

words.definition: To become wrinkled and small through dryness or cold.
words.sentence_usage: Without water, the flowers began to shrivel in the heat.

words.definition: Covered or hidden, often by mist, darkness, or cloth.
words.sentence_usage: The mountain peak was shrouded in cloud and mystery.

words.definition: Happening or done at the same time.
words.sentence_usage: There were simultaneous events taking place in different parts of the city.

words.definition: Bending in smooth curves.
words.sentence_usage: A sinuous trail wound through the reeds towards the old boathouse.

words.definition: Very thin or bare like a skeleton.
words.sentence_usage: Skeletal trees clawed at the winter sky with leafless branches.

words.definition: To suffocate; to cover thickly; to suppress.
words.sentence_usage: She tried to smother the flames with a damp cloth before they spread.

words.definition: Burning slowly with smoke but little flame.
words.sentence_usage: Smouldering logs glowed red in the hearth long after everyone had gone to bed.

words.definition: Enjoying the company of others; friendly.
words.sentence_usage: She was a very sociable person who made friends wherever she went.

words.definition: Serious and quiet.
words.sentence_usage: A solemn hush filled the chapel as the candles flickered.

words.definition: Thinly spread, with not much there.
words.sentence_usage: Sparse grass clung to the dry hillside between patches of stone.

words.definition: Relating to space and the arrangement of things within it.
words.sentence_usage: Architects need strong spatial awareness to design buildings effectively.

words.definition: A sample taken for examination; an example of something.
words.sentence_usage: The scientist collected a specimen of the rare plant for analysis.

words.definition: Very impressive to look at.
words.sentence_usage: Fireworks burst above the river in a spectacular shower of colour.

words.definition: Full of life, courage, or energy.
words.sentence_usage: The spirited pony tossed its mane and raced across the field.

words.definition: Very beautiful or excellent.
words.sentence_usage: What a splendid view stretched from the castle tower to the sea.

words.definition: Lively and full of energy.
words.sentence_usage: Despite his age, the gardener remained sprightly and quick on his feet.

words.definition: Walked in an unsteady way.
words.sentence_usage: The sailor staggered across the deck as the storm lifted the ship.

words.definition: Quiet and careful so nobody notices.
words.sentence_usage: A stealthy fox slipped through the moonlit garden without a sound.

words.definition: Uncomfortably hot or airless.
words.sentence_usage: The stifling attic trapped the heat of the afternoon like an oven.

words.definition: To encourage something to happen or develop.
words.sentence_usage: The exciting lesson helped to stimulate the pupils' interest in science.

words.definition: Strong, thick, and solid.
words.sentence_usage: A stout rope held the boat steady against the tug of the tide.

words.definition: Refusing to change your mind or give in.
words.sentence_usage: The stubborn goat planted its hooves and would not cross the bridge.

words.definition: Amazingly large or wonderful.
words.sentence_usage: The explorers gasped at the stupendous waterfall thundering into the gorge.

words.definition: Based on personal feelings or opinions rather than facts.
words.sentence_usage: Art appreciation is largely subjective — what one person loves another may dislike.

words.definition: A person or thing that takes the place of another.
words.sentence_usage: When the striker was injured, the manager brought on a substitute.

words.definition: Small or quiet in a clever way that is not obvious.
words.sentence_usage: A subtle smile flickered across her face when the riddle was solved.

words.definition: Following one after another in sequence.
words.sentence_usage: The team won three successive championships without a defeat.

words.definition: Enough for a particular purpose.
words.sentence_usage: She had sufficient time to complete the exam before the bell rang.

words.definition: Quietly bad-tempered and unhappy.
words.sentence_usage: Sulky after losing the game, he sat with folded arms by the window.

words.definition: Flexible and able to bend easily without breaking.
words.sentence_usage: Years of yoga kept her body supple and strong.

words.definition: Moved suddenly forwards with great force.
words.sentence_usage: Water surged through the broken gate and rushed across the yard.

words.definition: Feeling that something is not right.
words.sentence_usage: She gave the mysterious parcel a suspicious glance before touching it.

words.definition: Very fast.
words.sentence_usage: A swift shadow skimmed over the grass as the hawk dived.

words.definition: Twisted together in a messy knot.
words.sentence_usage: Tangled roots crawled across the path like sleeping snakes.

words.definition: A person skilled in practical or mechanical work in a specific field.
words.sentence_usage: The sound technician checked all the microphones before the concert.

words.definition: Full of moving life.
words.sentence_usage: The rock pool was teeming with darting fish and tiny waving crabs.

words.definition: Mild and moderate, not extreme; also showing self-restraint.
words.sentence_usage: Britain has a temperate climate, with warm summers and mild winters.

words.definition: Very stormy or full of strong emotion.
words.sentence_usage: The sea turned tempestuous, hurling foam over the harbour wall.

words.definition: Gentle, soft, and caring.
words.sentence_usage: With tender hands, she bandaged the robin's tiny wing.

words.definition: Stiff or worried because something might happen.
words.sentence_usage: A tense silence gripped the courtroom before the verdict was read.

words.definition: Careful not to waste money or supplies.
words.sentence_usage: The thrifty inventor saved every screw and spring for future projects.

words.definition: Very excited and pleased.
words.sentence_usage: She was thrilled to spot a dolphin leaping beside the ferry.

words.definition: Extremely loud like thunder.
words.sentence_usage: Thunderous applause shook the hall after the final note rang out.

words.definition: Shy and not very brave.
words.sentence_usage: The timid rabbit paused at the edge of the clearing before hopping out.

words.definition: Willing to accept behaviour or opinions different from one's own.
words.sentence_usage: A good teacher is tolerant of different learning styles and abilities.

words.definition: Very tall and impressive.
words.sentence_usage: Towering pines hemmed the path and blocked the fading light.

words.definition: Peaceful and calm.
words.sentence_usage: A tranquil hush settled over the snow-covered village.

words.definition: Allowing light to pass through; also easy to see through or understand.
words.sentence_usage: The glass was completely transparent, giving a clear view of the garden.

words.definition: Shaking slightly because of fear or emotion.
words.sentence_usage: In a tremulous voice, he read the final line of the letter.

words.definition: A shaky, nervous feeling that something scary is about to happen.
words.sentence_usage: As he pushed open the creaking door of the abandoned mansion, a wave of cold trepidation washed over him.

words.definition: Something said, done, or given to show respect or admiration.
words.sentence_usage: The performance was a tribute to the famous composer on his hundredth birthday.

words.definition: Feeling proud and happy after success.
words.sentence_usage: Triumphant cheers erupted when the puzzle box finally clicked open.

words.definition: Very noisy, disorderly, and full of wild confusion.
words.sentence_usage: The tumultuous protest surged through the square, with banners snapping above the roaring crowd.

words.definition: Very rough, disturbed, or troubled.
words.sentence_usage: The plane shook in the turbulent clouds above the mountains.

words.definition: The soft light just before night.
words.sentence_usage: In the purple twilight, the castle looked both beautiful and eerie.

words.definition: Something that seems to be everywhere you look.
words.sentence_usage: In the futuristic city, glowing neon signs were ubiquitous, illuminating every dark alley and towering skyscraper.

words.definition: Strangely unusual in a surprising way.
words.sentence_usage: There was an uncanny likeness between the portrait and the new visitor.

words.definition: Slightly worried or uncomfortable.
words.sentence_usage: An uneasy feeling prickled the back of her neck as the music stopped.

words.definition: Not recognised or known.
words.sentence_usage: An unidentified object was found near the school gates.

words.definition: Hard to control.
words.sentence_usage: Unruly curls bounced around his face no matter how much water he used.

words.definition: Not clear or exact.
words.sentence_usage: He gave a vague answer that solved nothing at all.

words.definition: Very brave and determined.
words.sentence_usage: The valiant knight rode towards the dragon despite the flames.

words.definition: A form or version of something that differs slightly from others.
words.sentence_usage: This dialect is a regional variant of standard English.

words.definition: Extremely large and wide.
words.sentence_usage: A vast plain stretched to the horizon beneath the silver moon.

words.definition: An arched roof or ceiling; a secure room for valuables; to jump over something.
words.sentence_usage: The athlete vaulted over the bar with ease.

words.definition: The edge or border of something; to be on the point of.
words.sentence_usage: She was on the verge of tears as she read the sad letter.

words.definition: Full of bright colour or energy.
words.sentence_usage: Vibrant banners fluttered above the street festival in the spring wind.

words.definition: Very cruel or violent.
words.sentence_usage: The vicious dog bared its teeth and snarled at the gate.

words.definition: Watchful and alert for danger.
words.sentence_usage: Vigilant guards paced the walls as darkness closed around the fort.

words.definition: Strong, healthy, and full of force.
words.sentence_usage: With vigorous strokes, she rowed the boat away from the rocks.

words.definition: Having high moral standards; righteous.
words.sentence_usage: The judge was known for making virtuous and fair decisions.

words.definition: Very bright, strong, or clear in the mind.
words.sentence_usage: He could still remember the vivid blue of the kingfisher's wings.

words.definition: Likely to change suddenly and become dangerous or violent very quickly.
words.sentence_usage: The border region remained volatile after the ceasefire, with every rumour threatening to spark fresh clashes.

words.definition: Super hungry, like you could eat absolutely everything in sight.
words.sentence_usage: After wandering in the wilderness for three days, the survivor ate the berries with a voracious appetite.

words.definition: Moved around without a clear plan.
words.sentence_usage: They wandered through the old lanes until they found the hidden square.

words.definition: In a careful and suspicious way.
words.sentence_usage: The fox warily approached the picnic basket, ready to flee at any sound.

words.definition: Very tired.
words.sentence_usage: Weary travellers sank onto the benches as the train steamed in.

words.definition: Playfully unusual and imaginative.
words.sentence_usage: A whimsical clockwork bird sang from the shelf every hour.

words.definition: Spinning around quickly.
words.sentence_usage: Whirling snowflakes danced in the lamplight like tiny white moths.

words.definition: A wild, natural area not changed much by humans.
words.sentence_usage: They hiked for miles through the mountain wilderness.

words.definition: Quietly sad because you want something you cannot have.
words.sentence_usage: She cast a wistful glance at the sea as the ship disappeared into fog.

words.definition: Dried up, shrivelled, or weakened.
words.sentence_usage: Withered petals carpeted the greenhouse floor after the heatwave.

words.definition: Very unhappy, poor, or unpleasant.
words.sentence_usage: Lost in the rain without a coat, he felt thoroughly wretched.

words.definition: Wanted something very much.
words.sentence_usage: She yearned to see the northern lights with her own eyes.

words.definition: Full of eager energy for something.
words.sentence_usage: A zealous volunteer stayed behind to stack every chair after the concert.
